driver.body: Everything above in Devices — the four form factors, the thin-on-purpose margin, the fleet-ridden supply chain — exists to serve one end: proprietary sensor data. Devices' claim on the moat is **breadth (B)**, the one term this domain owns, and this driver is where breadth stops being a slogan and becomes measurable value — value that lowers cost and raises trust across the other five domains' P&Ls rather than showing up in a device margin of its own [S-076].

mechanisms.body:
The manifold's payoff is realised in D6's models, and the couplings are specific [S-514]. **Kinematic** road-roughness data sharpens D1's routing and ride-comfort models and feeds D2's siting — rough corridors correlate with battery-drain hotspots worth a charger. **Acoustic** fault signatures drive predictive maintenance across the D1 fleet and the D2 charger network, converting reactive downtime into scheduled service. **Electrical** telemetry from the KN-2 feeds D2's battery-health and dispatch pricing. And the on-device secure element plus placement context feed D4's fraud model — a payment tap whose device reports an impossible kinematic trajectory is a fraud signal no card network can see.

The second data table quantifies the attributed lift — Kepler's internal model-improvement attribution from the sensor manifold, FY26 estimate [S-515]. That table is the answer to the board's fair challenge — **is the compounding real or a story?** Each row is a coupling the governing thought predicts, now carrying a number. Breadth (B) is not an abstraction; it is a **−18% downtime** figure and an **−11% fraud false-positive** figure that land on other domains' P&Ls, not on Devices' own [S-516].

The edge-case worth naming: the environmental modality produces a saleable data product — street-level air quality sold to municipalities — the one place Devices' data monetises directly rather than only through internal model lift. It is a hedge that gives the breadth thesis an external price signal to validate against, so the −18% and −11% attributions are not the only evidence the compounding is real.
